items.title: World loses patience with Trump as war is no longer just about Iran

children.content: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump speaks into the ear of Emmanual Macron, both of which are posing for a photo against a white backdrop

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump speaks into the ear of Emmanual Macron, both of which are posing for a photo against a white backdrop

items.title: Supermarkets asked to pay more for produce to absorb fuel costs for farmers

children.content: The effects of Australia's fuel shortage begin to compound for farmers, who have called on supermarkets to help relieve the pressure, while the government urges consumers not to panic-buy.

image.altText: A man in shorts walks into a modern-looking Woolworths shopfront.

items.title: Two arrested as part of investigation into movements of Dezi Freeman

children.content: Two people have been arrested as police investigate the movements of Dezi Freeman, who was fatally shot by police last week after months on the run.

image.altText: Man holding phone horizontally while wearing body camera on a property that looks like a farm with mountain in the background

items.title: Your pocket guide to daylight saving time ending in Australia

children.content: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

image.altText: A woman reaches out to an alarm clock from bed.

items.title: 'Extraordinary' images of home as astronauts pass halfway mark to Moon

children.content: As Artemis II astronauts forge ahead, NASA has released new images from inside the Orion spacecraft, including a portrait of Earth's deep blue oceans.

image.altText: The entire Earth is seen from space, with blue oceans and swirling tendrils of white clouds.

images.altText: An F-15 flies through the air

articles.title: Iran's downing of two US aircraft will shock Americans in three ways

children.content: This is the point when the US either goes deeper into this war or makes a clear decision to end it.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks to reporters in front of Air Force One

articles.title: The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf

children.content: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

image.altText: A satellite photo of a lot of large ships floating in the sea.

articles.title: How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

children.content: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil, like the Philippines in Southeast Asia, are most vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran.

image.altText: An older man in a white shirt leans against a bus.

children.content: Two US planes have been shot down by Iran with 2 crew members rescued while another remains missing. Follow live.

items.title: One crew member missing after two US jets shot down by Iran

updates.title: 'Disgusting': Prediction site under fire over bets on fate of missing US airman
Sailors trapped by Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade plead for help
Iranian official appears to mock the US after downing aircraft

items.title: Dragons booed off after horror first half

children.content: St George Illawarra's poor form has continued with the Red V faithful showing their displeasure by booing their team as the left the field at halftime. Follow live.

image.altText: A rugby league player in white and red charges through a tackle

items.title: Port poised for first away win of the season as Richmond wilts at the MCG

children.content: Richmond kick off Easter Saturday football at the MCG looking for their first win of the season against Port Adelaide, before the Eagles host the Swans in Perth. Follow live.

image.altText: A group of AFL players in blue and white celebrate a goal

items.title: Russia launches Good Friday daytime attacks on Ukraine, killing 14

children.content: The Russian military used more than 500 drones and dozens of missiles, according to the Ukrainian air force, destroying residential buildings and damaging a veterinary clinic.

image.altText: A damaged apartment building sits behind a rusty-looking metal swingset against a grey sky.

items.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence

children.content: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

image.altText: Ron Acfalle paddles a canoe.

items.title: Watch as Olympic silver medallist destroys the Stawell Gift field

children.content: Drone footage shows the US sprint superstar dominate her 120m sprint heat from the back marker.

image.altText: Richardson flies past Stawell Gift opponents

gallery.altText: Sha'Carri Richardson runs a long way ahead of an opponent at the Stawell Gift.

thumbnail.altText: Sha'Carri Richardson runs a long way ahead of an opponent at the Stawell Gift.

items.title: Police launch full-scale search to locate woman missing in popular tourism spot

children.content: A large-scale search is underway for 51-year-old Naomi Marie Duckett, who was last seen at Gap Natural Bridge within Torndirrup National Park just before midday on Thursday.

image.altText: A head shot of a 51-year-old woman with black top and grey eyes.

items.title: Sunken trawler alerts police to alleged drug-smuggling plan

children.content: Police have charged nine men over a scheme to allegedly smuggle tonnes of cocaine into Victoria by sea.

image.altText: A man in handcuffs with police.

items.title: A quick dinner with butter beans, leeks and spinach

children.content: This quick and easy butter bean recipe from Helen Tzouganatos might solve your dinner woes.

gallery.altText: A pink bowl of brothy beans with visible spinach, against a pink background.

thumbnail.altText: A pink bowl of brothy beans with visible spinach, against a pink background.

items.title: Gordi helped write a song for her partner, then 'stole it' back

children.content: Gordi's new single was originally meant to be for her partner. Thankfully, both artists know the importance of trusting their gut.

image.altText: Gordi wearing a shiny jacket and holding the collar while standing against a dark background with pink lighting.

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

children.content: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

image.altText: A close-up of a person's hand inside a red glove, holding a toilet brush to a toilet bowl

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

gallery.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

thumbnail.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

items.title: Who is eligible for the new nasal spray flu vaccine?

children.content: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

image.altText: A small child receives a nasal spray while sitting on a parent's lap

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

image.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: 'Adrenaline rush' of adaptive skateboarding redefines inclusive sport

children.content: Using a specially designed skate frame, adaptive skateboarding allows participants to stand safely while trained instructors guide and stabilise them.

gallery.altText: two people skateboarding using a harness.

thumbnail.altText: two people skateboarding using a harness.

items.title: How a rising teen star is making a name for himself in Aussie athletics

children.content: Teenagers Gout Gout and Cameron Myers are already creating an impact on the international stage, helping to inspire the likes of 18-year-old Daniel Williams to follow in their footsteps.

image.altText: Daniel Williams takes off his race bib to give to spectators at the 2026 Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: Why Stuart isn't alarmed by Raiders' poor start to NRL season

children.content: The Raiders have triumphed in only one of their opening four matches, but coach Ricky Stuart says the club has been in "worse positions than this".

image.altText: Raiders players look dejected during a 2026 NRL match.

items.title: Demons star praised for reaching 'another level' with career-best form

children.content: Ed Langdon says Melbourne teammate Kysaiah Pickett is flourishing in his midfield role, as the Demons continue life under new coach Steven King.

image.altText: Kysaiah Pickett of the Demons celebrates a goal during a 2026 AFL match against the Blues.

items.title: Cotton-Davis rivalry has gone global, 36ers coach says ahead of decider

children.content: As Sydney and Adelaide put the final touches on their preparations for this year's NBL championship decider, 36ers coach Mike Wells says he believes the rivalry his side has built with the Kings has attracted a global audience to the league.

image.altText: Mike Wells gestures from the sideline during Game 4 of the 2025/26 NBL championship series.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

gallery.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track

thumbnail.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track

items.title: Arnhem Land rock art raises questions about mainland Tasmanian tigers

children.content: Newly documented rock art in Arnhem Land has raised questions about how long Tasmanian tigers lived on the mainland before they were wiped out.

image.altText: Rock art depicting Tasmanian tigers

items.title: Study says economic value of grey-headed flying fox poo worth millions

children.content: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

image.altText: A grey-headed flying fox hanging from an aviary roof eating from a pink plant as it hangs upside down.

items.title: From farm to fashion festival: Indigenous designs stun on the runway

children.content: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.

image.altText: a woman walking down a runway wearing a long white dress featuring a feather waist.

items.title: These 'readers' created in remote NT are unlike those in most schools

children.content: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

image.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

items.title: This moon mission is different. They have toilets now

children.content: More than 50 years ago, the Apollo missions took humanity to the Moon. Now with Artemis, a new generation is about to be launched. What has changed — and not changed — between then and now might surprise you.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of woman with long hair, glasses and a long-sleeved dress next to a stack of bound papers as tall as her.

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of woman with long hair, glasses and a long-sleeved dress next to a stack of bound papers as tall as her.

avatar.alt: Close-up of person wearing dark top and green earrings.

items.title: After decades of success, love and loss, outback pub changes hands

children.content: After turning "the worst pub you ever walked into" into a tourism award winner, Stephen Burns is stepping away from managing the Nindigully Pub.

image.altText: An ariel view of the pub with some four wheel rives out front and campervans down the side.

items.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo

children.content: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

image.altText: A shot of a grey building with the words 'central law courts' written on the side.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon

children.content: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

gallery.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.

thumbnail.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.
